fanout_allowlist.name: Explore
fanout_allowlist.rationale: Harness definition excludes Edit/Write/NotebookEdit; existing deep-explore fanout baseline (accepted exposure, not new attack surface); doubles as the planner agent (only research-capable entry).
fanout_allowlist.residual_risk: Has Bash — could theoretically mutate files or git state. Mitigated by prompt read-only contract + run-verify.js git-scoped checks (porcelain + tracked/untracked content hashes/refs/config/worktree/stash + .git/ hooks & info/exclude digest). Out of v1 verification scope (v2 OQ): writes outside the repo; and index-mediated hiding of a tracked-file edit via `git update-index --assume-unchanged`/`--skip-worktree` (the edit then shows in neither `status --porcelain` nor `diff HEAD`, and the digest does not hash `.git/index`) — v2 must fold a stat-independent index digest in. (Packed-refs ref retarget/add/delete is already caught by refs_sha256's for-each-ref, so it is not a gap.)

explicit_exclusions.name: coverage-analyst
explicit_exclusions.reason: Bash(find:*) carries a write surface (-exec/-delete); verification-surface ⊇ attack-surface principle not satisfied for a new exposure.

explicit_exclusions.name: git-investigator
explicit_exclusions.reason: Bash(git:*) carries git write operations (tag/config/ref). run-verify.js refs/config hashes would detect them, but deny-by-default: new exposure without an existing fanout precedent is not worth the benefit. History archaeology runs as main-skill (/git-investigate) instead.
